verification_gate: Lifecycle fixtures prove Draft, blocked, Ready, running, Complete, and rolled-back projection; next_ready_task_id is null while the global coverage gate is closed.

gate_result.tdd_baseline: HEAD update-task-graph-status.py lumps every non-complete task into ready_task_ids and sets next_ready_task_id to a Draft task (LC-DRAFT in the fixture; LIFEOS-000 on the real graph).

proof_summary: GRAPH-004 corrects task lifecycle projection in the status projector. TDD baseline: the pre-change HEAD projector mislabels Draft/blocked/running/rolled-back tasks as ready (ready_task_ids=[LC-DRAFT, LC-BLOCKED, LC-READY, LC-RUNNING, LC-ROLLBACK]) and selects the Draft task LC-DRAFT as next_ready_task_id. The fix derives a canonical lifecycle_state per task: proof is authoritative (a passing ledger entry projects Complete, a failing/rolled-back entry reopens the task as rolled-back per 08_EXECUTION_GATES rule 5), otherwise the source status governs. The lifecycle fixture (generated/fixtures/lifecycle/) proves all six states project correctly: LC-DRAFT->draft, LC-BLOCKED->blocked, LC-READY->ready, LC-RUNNING->running, LC-COMPLETE->complete (ledger pass), LC-ROLLBACK->rolled-back (ledger fail). Only genuinely ready tasks enter ready_task_ids, so no non-complete task is mislabeled ready. next_ready_task_id is null while the global coverage gate is closed (the fail-closed default) and surfaces LC-READY only when --coverage-gate open. On the real 196-task graph the new projector keeps result=pass with 0 forbidden updates, reports next_ready_task_id=null and ready_count=0 (fail-closed, replacing the incorrect LIFEOS-000), and correctly reclassifies LPS-025 as rolled-back because its only ledger entry is a genuine fail. The change is additive: existing fields are preserved and lifecycle_state, lifecycle_counts, global_coverage_gate, and the per-state id lists are new. No product execution was performed.

live_commands.purpose: TDD baseline: pre-change projector mislabels Draft as ready and picks a Draft as next_ready
New projector, coverage gate closed (default): six lifecycle states correct, next_ready null
New projector, coverage gate open: only the genuinely ready task surfaces
Real graph stays valid: result pass, 0 forbidden, next_ready null while gate closed
output: lifecycle_counts: complete=112 draft=82 running=1 rolled-back=1 (LPS-025 has a genuine fail ledger entry)
